name: Kenya
welcome: Jambo! Welcome to Kenya — home of the greatest wildlife safari on Earth!
summary: Kenya is a beautiful country in East Africa, sitting right on the equator.
It is famous for its incredible wildlife — lions, elephants, giraffes, and zebras roam freely here.
The Masai Mara grasslands host the greatest animal migration on Earth every year.

LOCATION.intro: Kenya sits right in the middle of Africa — let's find it!

items.title: Kenya in Africa
narration: Kenya is in East Africa, right on the equator — which is the imaginary middle line of the Earth!
It borders the Indian Ocean, Tanzania, Uganda, Ethiopia, and other countries.
Standing on the equator in Kenya, you are exactly halfway between the North and South Poles!

ANIMALS.intro: Kenya has more amazing wild animals than almost anywhere else!

items.title: African Lion
narration: The lion is known as the King of the Jungle — though in Kenya it lives on the open grassland!
Male lions have magnificent manes of thick, golden fur.
Lions live in family groups called prides and are the only cats that do so.

items.title: African Elephant
narration: African elephants are the largest land animals on Earth!
They use their amazing trunks to drink, eat, and even hug each other.
Elephants never forget — they can remember friends and places for 50 years!

items.title: Giraffe
narration: Giraffes are the tallest animals in the world — up to 6 metres tall!
Their incredibly long necks help them reach leaves high in acacia trees.
Despite their huge size, giraffes only sleep about 30 minutes a day!

items.title: Cheetah
narration: The cheetah is the fastest land animal on Earth — it can reach 112 kilometres per hour!
It uses its speed to chase down prey like gazelles across the open plains.
Unlike other big cats, cheetahs cannot roar — instead they make a sound like a bird chirping!

items.title: Masai Mara Safari
narration: Every year, over two million wildebeest, zebras, and gazelles migrate across the Masai Mara!
This is called the Great Migration — the greatest wildlife spectacle on Earth.
Lions, cheetahs, and crocodiles wait to catch the herds as they cross the rivers.

items.title: Mount Kenya
narration: Mount Kenya is the second-highest mountain in Africa, reaching 5,199 metres!
Even though it is almost on the equator, its peaks are covered in snow.
The country of Kenya was named after this magnificent mountain.

PEOPLE.intro: Kenya is home to more than 40 different peoples and cultures!

items.title: Maasai People
narration: The Maasai are one of Kenya's most famous peoples, known for their bright red clothing!
They are semi-nomadic — they move with their cattle across the grasslands.
Maasai warriors are famous for their incredible jumping ability — they leap very high to show their strength!

FOOD.intro: Kenyan food is hearty, flavourful, and made with love!

items.title: Ugali
narration: Ugali is Kenya's most important staple food — made from maize flour and water.
It is cooked into a thick, stiff porridge that you eat with your hands.
Kenyans eat it almost every day, scooping up vegetables, beans, or meat with it.

items.title: Nyama Choma
narration: Nyama Choma means roasted meat in Swahili — and it is Kenya's favourite dish!
Meat is grilled over an open fire until it is perfectly charred and juicy.
Eating nyama choma with friends and family is a big celebration in Kenya.

FUN_FACTS.intro: Kenya is full of remarkable facts!

items.title: World's Fastest Runners
narration: Kenya produces the world's fastest long-distance runners!
Kenyan athletes have won more Olympic marathon medals than any other nation.
The Rift Valley in Kenya is called the cradle of world running champions.

items.title: Flamingo Lake
narration: Lake Nakuru in Kenya turns bright PINK — because of millions of flamingos!
Up to two million flamingos gather there, making it look like a pink carpet.
It is one of the most spectacular wildlife scenes in the world.
